1774796027469.title: Introduction to Photosynthesis
1774796027469.description: Learn how plants convert sunlight and CO₂ into glucose and oxygen.
1774796027469.subject: Biology
1774796027469.transcript: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to produce oxygen and energy in the form of glucose. This process occurs primarily in the chloroplasts of plant cells, where the green pigment chlorophyll absorbs light energy.

1774796028995.title: The Water Cycle Explained
1774796028995.description: How water moves through Earths systems — evaporation, condensation, precipitation.
1774796028995.subject: Geography
1774796028995.transcript: The water cycle describes how water evaporates from the surface of the earth, rises into the atmosphere, cools and condenses into rain or snow in clouds, and falls again to the surface as precipitation.

1774796029008.title: Newton's Three Laws of Motion
1774796029008.description: An accessible text explanation of Newton's fundamental laws of motion.
1774796029008.subject: Physics
1774796029008.textContent:
Newton's Three Laws of Motion

1. First Law (Inertia)
An object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion, unless acted upon by an external force. This is why passengers lurch forward when a car brakes suddenly.

2. Second Law (F = ma)
Force equals mass times acceleration. A heavier object requires more force to accelerate at the same rate as a lighter one. This explains why it's harder to push a heavy box than a light one.

3. Third Law (Action-Reaction)
For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. When you push against a wall, the wall pushes back with equal force. Rockets work by expelling gas downward, which propels the rocket upward.

1774796029018.title: Introduction to Algebra
1774796029018.description: Understand variables, expressions, and basic algebraic equations.
1774796029018.subject: Mathematics
1774796029018.transcript: Algebra is a branch of mathematics dealing with symbols and the rules for manipulating those symbols. In elementary algebra, those symbols represent quantities without fixed values, known as variables. Let x represent an unknown number. If x plus 3 equals 7, then x must equal 4.

child_lesson_1.title: Learn Colors for Kids
child_lesson_1.description: A fun interactive way for kids to learn basic colors like Red, Blue, and Green.
child_lesson_1.subject: Early Learning
child_lesson_1.transcript: Look at the colors! Here is Red. The apple is red. Here is Blue. The sky is blue. Here is Green. The grass is green. Red, blue, green! Great job learning your colors.

child_lesson_2.title: Animals and Their Sounds
child_lesson_2.description: Learn about different animals and the sounds they make.
child_lesson_2.subject: Early Learning
child_lesson_2.transcript: Let's learn animals! The cow says moo. The dog says woof. The cat says meow. Moo, woof, meow! Animals are fun.

child_lesson_3.title: Counting 1 to 5
child_lesson_3.description: Practice counting numbers one through five.
child_lesson_3.subject: Math
child_lesson_3.transcript: Let's count together! One, two, three, four, five. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Awesome counting!

child_lesson_4.title: The Solar System for Kids
child_lesson_4.description: Learn about the sun and the 8 planets in our solar system!
child_lesson_4.subject: Science
child_lesson_4.transcript: Welcome to space! At the center of our solar system is the Sun. Orbiting the Sun are eight planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.

child_lesson_5.title: The Seven Continents
child_lesson_5.description: A fun geographical song to remember the 7 continents of the Earth.
child_lesson_5.subject: Geography
child_lesson_5.transcript: Let's travel the world! The seven continents are North America, South America, Europe, Africa, Asia, Australia, and Antarctica.

child_lesson_6.title: Phonics Song (A to Z)
child_lesson_6.description: Learn the sounds of the English alphabet.
child_lesson_6.subject: English
child_lesson_6.transcript: A is for Apple, a-a-apple. B is for Ball, b-b-ball. C is for Cat, c-c-cat. D is for Dog, d-d-dog.

child_lesson_7.title: Learning Shapes
child_lesson_7.description: Identify basic geometry shapes in our everyday world.
child_lesson_7.subject: Math
child_lesson_7.transcript: A circle is round like a ball. A square has four equal sides. A triangle has three sides. Look around and see what shapes you can find!

child_lesson_8.title: Ancient Egypt for Kids
child_lesson_8.description: Discover the pyramids, pharaohs, and the Nile river.
child_lesson_8.subject: History
child_lesson_8.transcript: Ancient Egypt was a civilization in North Africa along the Nile River. They built giant pyramids as tombs for their rulers, known as Pharaohs.
